title: Onboarding Checklist
markdown:
# Onboarding Checklist

All new Q employees must complete onboarding within their first two weeks.

## Pre-Start (HR)

- Send employment contract and equipment request form
- Create Google Workspace and Slack accounts
- Add new joiner to BambooHR

## Day 1

- Welcome session with People team (30 min)
- IT setup: laptop, MFA, VPN, password manager
- Review Company Handbook and sign policy acknowledgements

## Week 1

- Meet your buddy and line manager
- Complete mandatory compliance training (GDPR, Security Awareness)
- Set up timesheet access in Harvest

## Week 2

- Department-specific tooling walkthrough
- First 1:1 with line manager to set 30-60-90 day goals
- Submit onboarding feedback form to People team

title: Leave and Absence Policy
markdown:
# Leave and Absence Policy

## Annual Leave

Full-time employees receive 25 days of annual leave plus public holidays. Leave must be requested in BambooHR at least 2 weeks in advance for periods of 3+ consecutive days.

## Sick Leave

Notify your line manager before 09:30 on the first day of absence. Log sick days in BambooHR within 48 hours. A medical certificate is required for absences exceeding 3 consecutive days.

## Parental Leave

Primary carers receive 16 weeks paid parental leave. Secondary carers receive 4 weeks. Notify HR at least 8 weeks before the expected start date.

## Unpaid Leave

Unpaid leave may be granted at manager discretion for exceptional circumstances. Submit a request via BambooHR with business justification.

title: Remote Work Guidelines
markdown:
# Remote Work Guidelines

Q operates a hybrid-first model: minimum 2 days per week in the office unless role-specific exceptions apply.

## Eligibility

All employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing probation. Fully remote arrangements require VP approval.

## Expectations

- Core hours: 10:00–16:00 in your local timezone
- Camera on for client calls and internal standups
- Maintain a professional, distraction-free workspace

## Equipment

Q provides a laptop, monitor allowance (€200/year), and home internet stipend (€50/month). Equipment remains company property.

## Security

Use company VPN when accessing internal systems from public networks. Do not share your screen with confidential client data visible.

title: Git Workflow
markdown:
# Git Workflow

All Q engineering projects follow trunk-based development with short-lived feature branches.

## Branch Naming

- `feature/JIRA-123-short-description`
- `fix/JIRA-456-bug-description`
- `chore/JIRA-789-task-description`

## Pull Requests

- Maximum PR lifetime: 3 days
- Minimum 1 approval from a code owner
- All CI checks must pass before merge
- Squash merge to main

## Commit Messages

Use conventional commits: `type(scope): description`

Types: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, ci

Example: `feat(auth): add OAuth2 login flow`

## Protected Branches

`main` and `release/*` branches require PR review and passing CI. Direct pushes are blocked.

title: Code Review Standards
markdown:
# Code Review Standards

Code review is mandatory for all changes to production codebases.

## Reviewer Responsibilities

- Respond to review requests within 4 business hours
- Check for correctness, security, test coverage, and readability
- Use constructive, specific feedback
- Approve only when you would be comfortable maintaining the code

## Author Responsibilities

- Keep PRs under 400 lines of changed code where possible
- Include a clear description and test plan
- Respond to feedback within 1 business day
- Do not merge your own PR without a second reviewer for critical systems

## Automated Checks

All PRs must pass: linting, unit tests, SAST scan (SonarQube), and dependency audit before human review.

title: Testing Requirements
markdown:
# Testing Requirements

## Coverage Targets

- Unit test coverage: minimum 80% for new code
- Critical paths (auth, payments, data processing): 95%
- Integration tests required for all API endpoints

## Test Types

| Type | When Required | Tooling |
|------|--------------|--------|
| Unit | All business logic | Jest, pytest, Go test |
| Integration | API boundaries | Supertest, httpx |
| E2E | User-facing flows | Playwright, Cypress |
| Load | High-traffic endpoints | k6 |

## CI Pipeline

Tests run on every PR. Flaky tests must be fixed or quarantined within 48 hours — never silenced permanently.

## Test Data

Never use production data in test environments. Use anonymised fixtures or factories.

title: API Design Standards
markdown:
# API Design Standards

All Q APIs follow REST conventions with OpenAPI 3.1 documentation.

## URL Structure

- Version prefix: `/api/v1/`
- Plural nouns: `/api/v1/projects`, `/api/v1/users`
- Nested resources max 2 levels deep

## Response Format

```json
{
  "data": {},
  "meta": { "page": 1, "total": 100 },
  "errors": []
}
```

## Status Codes

- 200: Success (GET, PUT, PATCH)
- 201: Created (POST)
- 204: No Content (DELETE)
- 400: Validation error
- 401: Unauthenticated
- 403: Forbidden
- 404: Not found
- 429: Rate limited

## Authentication

Use Bearer JWT tokens. Token expiry: 1 hour access, 7 day refresh. All endpoints except `/health` and `/auth/*` require authentication.

## Rate Limiting

Default: 100 requests/minute per API key. Burst: 200. Return `Retry-After` header on 429.

title: Security Standards
markdown:
# Security Standards

## Secrets Management

- Never commit secrets to git. Use environment variables or AWS Secrets Manager.
- Rotate API keys every 90 days.
- Pre-commit hooks must include secret scanning (gitleaks).

## Dependency Management

- Run `npm audit` / `pip audit` weekly
- Critical CVEs must be patched within 48 hours
- Pin dependency versions in production

## Data Classification

| Level | Examples | Handling |
|-------|----------|----------|
| Public | Marketing content | No restrictions |
| Internal | Process docs, code | Company access only |
| Confidential | Client data, PII | Encrypted at rest and in transit |
| Restricted | Payment data, credentials | Encrypted + access logging |

## Incident Response

Report security incidents to security@q.agency within 1 hour. Do not attempt to investigate alone. Preserve logs and affected systems.

title: Project Kickoff Process
markdown:
# Project Kickoff Process

Every new client project follows a standardised kickoff within 5 business days of contract signature.

## Pre-Kickoff Checklist

- Assign Project Manager and Tech Lead
- Create Jira project and Confluence space
- Set up GitHub repository from org template
- Schedule internal kickoff (delivery team only)

## Client Kickoff Agenda

1. Introductions and roles (15 min)
2. Project scope and success criteria (30 min)
3. Timeline and milestones (20 min)
4. Communication plan and tools (15 min)
5. Risks and assumptions (15 min)
6. Next steps and action items (15 min)

## Deliverables

- Signed Statement of Work (SoW)
- RACI matrix
- RAID log initialized
- Sprint 0 plan approved by client

title: Sprint Ceremonies
markdown:
# Sprint Ceremonies

Q delivery teams use 2-week sprints unless the client contract specifies otherwise.

## Sprint Planning (Monday, Week 1)

- Duration: 2 hours max
- Team selects items from prioritised backlog
- Each story must have acceptance criteria and estimate
- Sprint goal agreed and documented in Jira

## Daily Standup

- Duration: 15 minutes, same time daily
- Format: What I did / What I'll do / Blockers
- Blockers escalated to PM within 1 hour

## Sprint Review (Friday, Week 2)

- Duration: 1 hour
- Demo completed work to stakeholders
- Collect feedback for backlog refinement

## Retrospective (Friday, Week 2)

- Duration: 1 hour, after review
- Format: Start / Stop / Continue
- Action items assigned with owners and due dates

title: Client Communication Standards
markdown:
# Client Communication Standards

## Status Reports

Weekly status reports are due every Friday by 16:00 to the client PM. Template is in Confluence.

Required sections: Progress this week, Plan for next week, Risks/blockers, Budget burn rate.

## Escalation Path

1. Team level: Developer → Tech Lead (same day)
2. Project level: Tech Lead → Project Manager (within 4 hours)
3. Account level: PM → Account Director (within 24 hours)
4. Executive level: Account Director → Client executive sponsor

## Meeting Notes

All client meetings must have notes published within 24 hours. Store in the project Confluence space. Tag action items with owners and due dates.

## Response Times

- Client Slack messages: 4 business hours
- Client emails: 1 business day
- Production incidents: 30 minutes (see Incident Management)

title: Incident Management
markdown:
# Incident Management

## Severity Levels

| Level | Description | Response Time | Example |
|-------|-------------|---------------|--------|
| P1 | Production down | 15 min | Site unreachable |
| P2 | Major feature broken | 30 min | Payment failures |
| P3 | Minor feature degraded | 4 hours | Slow reports |
| P4 | Cosmetic / low impact | Next sprint | UI alignment |

## Response Process

1. Acknowledge alert in PagerDuty
2. Create incident channel: `#inc-YYYY-MM-DD-short-name`
3. Assign Incident Commander (IC) — usually on-call engineer
4. Post initial status update within response time SLA
5. Resolve and document post-mortem within 5 business days

## Post-Mortem Template

Required for all P1 and P2 incidents. Blameless format: Timeline, Root cause, Impact, Action items. Store in Confluence under project space.

title: Expense Policy
markdown:
# Expense Policy

## Submission

Submit expenses via Expensify within 30 days of incurring the cost. Late submissions may be rejected.

## Approval

- Under €100: Auto-approved with receipt
- €100–€500: Line manager approval
- Over €500: Finance team approval

## Allowable Expenses

- Client travel (pre-approved in writing)
- Conference tickets (with manager approval)
- Team meals (max €50/person)
- Software subscriptions for work use

## Non-Allowable

- Personal travel or meals
- Alcohol (unless client entertainment, pre-approved)
- Traffic fines or personal insurance

## Per Diem

Domestic travel: €35/day. International travel: €55/day. Covers meals and incidentals.

title: Timesheet Guidelines
markdown:
# Timesheet Guidelines

All billable and non-billable hours must be logged in Harvest daily.

## Rules

- Log time daily — never batch more than 1 week
- Minimum entry: 0.25 hours (15 minutes)
- Use correct project codes (see project list in Harvest)
- Internal meetings: use `INT-ADMIN` or `INT-RND` codes

## Categories

| Code | Use For |
|------|--------|
| DEV | Development work |
| QA | Testing and QA |
| PM | Project management |
| DES | Design work |
| INT-ADMIN | Internal admin meetings |
| INT-RND | R&D and learning time |
| LEAVE | Annual/sick leave |

## Approval

Timesheets are approved weekly by line managers. Submit by Monday 12:00 for the previous week.

title: Cloud Infrastructure Standards
markdown:
# Cloud Infrastructure Standards

Q standardises on AWS for cloud infrastructure. All resources must be provisioned via Terraform.

## Account Structure

- Production, Staging, Development in separate AWS accounts
- Use AWS Organizations for billing consolidation
- IAM roles follow least-privilege principle

## Naming Convention

`{env}-{project}-{service}-{resource}`

Example: `prod-acme-api-rds`

## Tagging

Required tags: `Environment`, `Project`, `Owner`, `CostCenter`, `ManagedBy=terraform`

## Monitoring

- CloudWatch alarms for all production services
- Datadog for APM and log aggregation
- PagerDuty integration for P1/P2 alerts

## Backups

- RDS: daily automated backups, 30-day retention
- S3: versioning enabled on all buckets
- Disaster recovery RTO: 4 hours, RPO: 1 hour

title: Data Privacy and GDPR
markdown:
# Data Privacy and GDPR

Q is GDPR-compliant. All employees must complete annual GDPR training.

## Personal Data Handling

- Collect only data necessary for the stated purpose
- Store PII encrypted at rest (AES-256)
- Retain client data only for the contract duration + 12 months
- Delete data upon client request within 30 days

## Data Subject Requests

Forward all access/deletion requests to privacy@q.agency within 24 hours. Legal team responds within 30 days.

## Data Processing Agreements

All client contracts must include a DPA. Template is in the Legal Confluence space. Do not modify without Legal review.

## Breach Notification

Report suspected data breaches to privacy@q.agency and security@q.agency immediately. GDPR requires notification to authorities within 72 hours.
